* [Send us an email](javascript:netapp_mailto())![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/pdf-icon.png)PDFs![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/dropdown-arrow.svg)* [![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/pdf-icon.png)PDF of this doc site](/us-en/ontap/pdfs/fullsite-sidebar/ONTAP_9_documentation.pdf)[![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/pdf-zip.png)```
Collection of separate PDF docs
```](#)# Creating your file...This may take a few minutes. Thanks for your patience.CancelYour file is readyOKAfter you upgrade ONTAP, there are several tasks you should perform to verify your cluster readiness.1. [Verify your cluster](task_verify_cluster_after_upgrade.html).After you upgrade ONTAP, you should verify your cluster version, cluster health, and storage health. If you are using a MetroCluster FC configuration, you also need to verify that the cluster is enabled for automatic unplanned switchover.
2. [Verify that all LIFs are on home ports](task_enabling_and_reverting_lifs_to_home_ports_post_upgrade.html).During a reboot, some LIFs might have been migrated to their assigned failover ports. After you upgrade a cluster, you must enable and revert any LIFs that are not on their home ports.
3. Verify [special considerations](concept_special_configurations_post_checks.html) specific to your cluster.If certain configurations exist on your cluster, you might need to perform additional steps after you upgrade.
4. [Update the Disk Qualification Package (DQP)](concept_when_you_need_to_update_the_disk_qualification_package.html).The DQP is not updated as part of an ONTAP upgrade.

key_values.title: What to do after an ONTAP upgrade)
